title: digitaria corymbosa subsp. roxburghiana
intro: Digitaria corymbosa subsp. roxburghiana is a sub species of the Digitaria corymbosa plant, commonly known as a type of grass native to India and other parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Digitaria corymbosa subsp. roxburghiana include its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as a natural antioxidant. Digitaria corymbosa subsp. roxburghiana is used in herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are prepared by boiling the plant material in water to release its bioactive compounds.
